scope.summary: Implement a garment/trim isolate-selection toggle in the Tailor viewport renderer: when active, only the selected garment or trim body is rendered (all others hidden); toggling off restores the full scene; exposed as a stable front-end control for parallel-model interaction and as a capture_view named-camera variant
acceptance_criteria: A toggle or named-camera option isolate_selection=true causes the renderer to draw only meshes whose garment_id or trim_id is in the current selection set
All other garments and trims receive draw_call=false (not removed from scene graph); toggling back restores all draw calls
The isolate state is reflected in capture_view (MT-264): passing isolate_selection=true in the camera spec isolates the named garment in the headless render
The isolate control has a stable HTML data-testid so parallel-model sessions can toggle it without screen-scraping
Isolate does not affect simulation state: all garment particles continue to simulate while isolated (display-only filter)
scope.risk_if_missed: Complex scenes with multiple layered garments and many trims become visually unnavigable; models using capture_view to inspect trim placement cannot isolate the target garment from background noise, degrading the autonomous quality loop for multi-garment outfits

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
